description: Mismatch between subject and verb number
Incorrect use of present perfect tense

example_incorrect: John told Bill that he was wrong
example_correct: John told Bill that Bill was wrong

description: Excessively long sentences without proper punctuation
example_incorrect: The study shows that students learn better when they have good teachers and the environment is supportive and resources are available
example_correct: The study shows that students learn better when they have good teachers, supportive environments, and available resources
Because of the results, we can conclude

description: Lack of parallel structure in lists
example_incorrect: Students like reading, writing, and to speak
example_correct: Students like reading, writing, and speaking

example_incorrect: I studied hard, but I failed
example_correct: I studied hard, yet I failed

example_incorrect: Although I studied, but I failed

description: Use of words with incorrect meaning
example_correct: The study was very literal (should be 'comprehensive')

description: Lack of logical connection between ideas
example_incorrect: Students study hard. The weather is nice
example_correct: Students study hard to achieve good grades

description: Ideas not relevant to the topic
example_correct: Essay about education focuses on learning
First point. Furthermore, the second point
The essay begins with clear thesis statement
